nodes.label: S6-D1: pin the manifest-list digest, proven through the real code path
nodes.rationale: Pin the platform-independent manifest-list digest captured from a fresh local pull (works on x86 and ARM; platform-specific digest would break ARM Macs). Rejected: keeping drifting tags (reopens the supply-chain hole) and lockfiles/remote pin fetch (machinery without payoff for two strings). Capture is not done until the exact refs are proven through the real preflight + --pull never run path. Trade-off stated honestly: pinned bytes get no upstream security fixes until a reviewed re-pin.

nodes.label: S6-D2: unpinned image overrides warn loudly, never refuse
nodes.rationale: Overriding images is a legitimate power feature (custom toolchains, air-gapped mirrors) — refusal would break real workflows; silence would violate 'misconfiguration is never silent'. One stderr warning per selection pass, covering both configured refs since selection only probes the python image.

nodes.label: S6-D3: no setup/doctor CLI in M1 — the first tool call is the doctor
nodes.rationale: A standalone checker would duplicate the report surface and add a maintenance promise; the MCP-first path already self-diagnoses — every failure returns the exact fix, and the S5-D1 cooldown makes fix-then-retry work in-session. Deferred as an M4 candidate.

nodes.label: S6-D4: proof gate runs under both Docker Desktop image stores
nodes.rationale: Proving pins only on whichever store mode this machine is in leaves the other mainstream mode (classic vs containerd) claimed but untested. The capture-day gate runs twice, once per store mode; ~15 minutes of manual toggling settles empirically what the draft settled by argument. If either mode fails, explicit support narrowing comes back as a fresh decision — it does not ship on hope.

nodes.label: Tag-drift trap: pull-by-tag no longer satisfies a digest preflight
nodes.rationale: Once the default is image@sha256:X, a plain docker pull of the tag can fetch digest Y after Docker Hub re-points the tag — the image looks present but preflight still fails, and a user following a stale instruction loops forever. Mitigation: every pull instruction, in Blocked messages and docs, uses the full digest-pinned reference verbatim — validated, not trusted, via the S6-D1/S6-D4 gate.
